content.full_text:
November 28, 1990

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Acting General Council
c/o Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: REQUEST FOR ADVICE LETTER

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I am the City Attorney for the City of Foster City. Councilmen Roger Chin serves as the City's Representative in the body known as the Airport Round Table. This is a group that consists of Representatives from the San Francisco Airport Commission, the Mayor of San Francisco, the Board of Supervisors of San Francisco, the Board of Supervisors of San Mateo County, and eight cities located in San Mateo County. In addition, there is an Ad Hoc Committee which consists of Representatives from the Federal Aeronautics Administration, the Airlines Association, and the Chief Pilots of the Airlines. The purpose and function of the Airport Round Table and the Ad Hoc Committee is to discuss problems such as noise and traffic and attempt to find solutions. The group was voluntarily organized and is not mandated to exist by any law. They do not have any power to require that anything be done.

Councilmen Chin is an architect with offices in the City of San Francisco. He received an inquiry from the Airport Administration Planning and Facilities Group as to whether he would like to be considered to do architectural work for the airport and/or its tenants. Councilmen Chin is concerned as to whether or not he would have a conflict of interest that would prevent his performing work for the airport and/or its tenants.

It is respectfully requested that you issue an advice letter as to whether or not a conflict of interests exists.

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
November 28, 1990
Page 2

Thank you for your help.

Very truly yours,

KENNETH M. DICKERSON
City Attorney

KMD:cc
cd428
